'新潟県小千谷市桜町1314', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '新潟県小千谷市大字桜町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊米神社八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11379707}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu |画像= [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|270px|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]]
Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いめしんしやはちまんくう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:小千谷市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'新潟県小千谷市桜町1314', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '新潟県小千谷市大字桜町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊米神社八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11379707}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu |画像= [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|270px|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]]
Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いめしんしやはちまんくう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:小千谷市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'新潟県小千谷市桜町1314', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '新潟県小千谷市大字桜町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊米神社八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11379707}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu |画像= [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|270px|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]]
Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いめしんしやはちまんくう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:小千谷市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'新潟県小千谷市桜町1314', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '新潟県小千谷市大字桜町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊米神社八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11379707}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu |画像= [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|270px|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]]
Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いめしんしやはちまんくう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:小千谷市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu |画像= [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|270px|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]]
Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いめしんしやはちまんくう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:小千谷市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002|1=Uonuma district}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404|1=1064}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen|1=Yamato Takeru}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin|1=Takeminakata}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine|1=Ukanomitama}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414|1=Amida-dō}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851|1=Fujiwara no Kanehira}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いめしんしやはちまんくう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:小千谷市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '新潟県小千谷市大字桜町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊米神社八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11379707}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu |画像= [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|270px|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]]
Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002|1=Uonuma district}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404|1=1064}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen|1=Yamato Takeru}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin|1=Takeminakata}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine|1=Ukanomitama}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414|1=Amida-dō}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851|1=Fujiwara no Kanehira}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いめしんしやはちまんくう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:小千谷市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '新潟県小千谷市大字桜町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊米神社八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11379707}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu |画像= [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|270px|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]]
Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002|1=Uonuma district}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404|1=1064}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen|1=Yamato Takeru}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin|1=Takeminakata}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine|1=Ukanomitama}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414|1=Amida-dō}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851|1=Fujiwara no Kanehira}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いめしんしやはちまんくう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:小千谷市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '新潟県小千谷市大字桜町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊米神社八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11379707}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu |画像= [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|270px|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]]
Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002|1=Uonuma district}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404|1=1064}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen|1=Yamato Takeru}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin|1=Takeminakata}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine|1=Ukanomitama}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414|1=Amida-dō}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851|1=Fujiwara no Kanehira}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いめしんしやはちまんくう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:小千谷市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '新潟県小千谷市大字桜町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊米神社八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11379707}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ime Shrine & Hachimangu |画像= [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|270px|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]]
Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002|1=Uonuma district}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404|1=1064}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen|1=Yamato Takeru}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin|1=Takeminakata}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine|1=Ukanomitama}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414|1=Amida-dō}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851|1=Fujiwara no Kanehira}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:小千谷市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Shrine (2), Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|270px|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]]
Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002|1=Uonuma district}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404|1=1064}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen|1=Yamato Takeru}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin|1=Takeminakata}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine|1=Ukanomitama}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414|1=Amida-dō}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851|1=Fujiwara no Kanehira}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haiden and temizuya |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}Sakuramachi 1314 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} disputed identification |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ime Shrine
Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ime-jinja-hachimangu Shrine, Ojiya, Niigata, Japan, August 2023.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] '''Ime Shrine & Hachimangu''' (いめじんじゃはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakuramachi, {{ill|lt=Ojiya|Q819174|WD=Q819174|1=Ojiya, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for "Ime Shrine," recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as one of the five seats in {{ill|lt=Uonuma District|Q11674002|WD=Q11674002|1=Uonuma district}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} * Betsumitama Ime-Ōkami (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * Tsushima-hime-no-Mikoto (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (in ''Echigo no Kuni Shikinaijinja Kō'') * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (in ''Onko no Shiori'') * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (co-enshrined) === History === Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was ordered to pacify {{ill|lt=Koshi Province|Q1351611|WD=Q1351611|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}} and arrived in this area. He first lived in Kami-Ime (Ryōshinden, now in Ojiya), later in Shimo-Ime (Sakuramachi), and then in the area called Imegahara, where present-day Ime Hachimangu stands. ''Ime Hachimangu Koshōsho-jō'' records: "Ime-Ōkami is Imehiko-no-Mikoto and settled here; therefore this place is called Imehara. He had one son and one daughter. The elder was named Kamiine Nagaho, and the younger princess was called Imetara-hime... The place where Ime Nagaho-ō sat is now called Kami-Ime, and the place where Imetara-hime sat is called Shimo-Ime." It is also said that while he lived here he was called Tegurihiko-no-Mikoto. When Minamoto no Yoshiie passed through this area, he fought the local Emishi. His retainer Nagano hid Yoshiie beneath Tōguni Bridge and saved his life. Yoshiie enshrined the protective principal image from inside his helmet as Hachimangu and allowed the Nagano family to govern the area. Another account says that in {{ill|lt=1064|Q19404|WD=Q19404|1=1064}}, when Yoshiie returned from Ōshū to Kyoto, he gave thanks for victory and left two retainers (Nagano and Abe, or Nakamachi and Nagano) to guard Hachiman Shrine. After that, Hachimangu was enshrined together with Ime Shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Daigongen|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (Ryūagaru, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Toyoki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|lt=Utsunomiya Shrine|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} (Shinbo, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Kusanagi Daigongen|1=Yamato Takeru}} (Kusanagi, Ichinomiya village) * Haguro Daigongen (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin|1=Takeminakata}} (Aburashinden, Ichinomiya village) * Yamamiya Suwa (Miyayama, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yamada, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainetama Shrine|1=Ukanomitama}} (Haguro, Ichinomiya village) * {{ill|lt=Amida-dō|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414|1=Amida-dō}} (formerly at the site of present Ojiya Nishi High School) ** Ichinomiya village corresponds to the present Sakuramachi area. === Kami-Ime Springs === There are two springs: Kami-Ime Spring and Shimo-Ime Spring. * It is said that drinking while praying to the deity of Ime drives away evil influences and disasters. * It is said that Kami-Ime Spring helps the left eye, and Shimo-Ime Spring helps the right eye. * It is said that men are healed by drinking Kami-Ime Spring, and women by drinking Shimo-Ime Spring. * A legend says that when the wife of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|WD=Q2994735|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kanehira|Q106234851|WD=Q106234851|1=Fujiwara no Kanehira}} were headed to Yoshidani in Ojiya and had no water, Kanehira faced toward Kyoto and prayed to {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}: "Please forgive the sins of the Fujiwara clan and grant us water." Water then welled up. This came to be called "Water of Deep Emotion," though it is also said the story arose because "Kami-Ime" and "kanmei" sound similar. === Shrine buildings === * Honden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** South-facing round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 1 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 1 ken 1 shaku * Haiden ** Round pillars ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 4 ken ** Depth: 4 ken * Heiden ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Thatched roof ** Frontage: 2 ken 1 shaku ** Depth: 2.5 ken === References === {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} * ''Ime Jinja Shashi'' (Ime Shrine history), Ime Shrine History Editorial Committee. * Igarashi Hidetarō, ''Legends of Ojiya'', Kōbunsha. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ime Shrine & Hachimangu]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]
